name: Picasso Trail
name_original: Paso dels Gosolans y Comabona desde Gósol — Parque Natural del Cadí-Moixeró
description: Tackle the difficult 11.8-mile hike to Paso dels Gosolans and Comabona Summit in Cadí-Moixeró Natural Park for stunning panoramic views.
region_intro: Dog friendly hiking trails in Cadí-Moixeró traverse a diverse landscape within the Catalan Pyrenees, spanning over 41,000 hectares. The region is characterized by the Cadí and Moixeró mountain ranges, which feature peaks up to 2,650 meters, alongside extensive pine forests, meadows, and rocky environments. Notable natural features include the iconic Pedraforca massif and water sources like the Llobregat River. Best dog friendly hiking trails in Cadí-Moixeró The most popular dog friendly hiking route is Castellar de n'Hug – Source of the Llobregat River loop from Castellar de n'Hug, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 35 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the scenic source of the Llobregat River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castellar de n'Hug – Coll de la Creueta loop from Castellar de n'Hug, a difficult 11.1 miles (17.9 km) path. This route offers significant elevation gain and panoramic views from Coll de la Creueta. Local hikers also love the Castellar de n'Hug – Font dels Torrents loop from Castellar de n'Hug, a 3.5 miles (5.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 10 minutes. Hiking in Cadí-Moixeró is defined by high mountain ranges, dense coniferous forests, and significant water features. The extensive network of trails offers options for different ability levels, from moderate forest walks to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Cadí-Moixeró are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 350 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Cadí-Moixeró's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many dog-friendly hiking trails are available in Cadí-Moixeró?
A: Cadí-Moixeró Natural Park offers a good selection of routes suitable for hiking with your dog. This guide features 12 dog-friendly trails, ranging from moderate to difficult, ensuring there's something for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any specific rules or permits required for hiking with dogs in Cadí-Moixeró Natural Park?
A: While specific permits for general hiking with dogs are not typically required, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas and near livestock, to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers and animals. Always clean up after your dog.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike with my dog in Cadí-Moixeró?
A: The best time to hike with your dog in Cadí-Moixeró is generally during spring, early summer, and autumn. During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the trails are less likely to be affected by snow or extreme heat. Summer can be pleasant at higher altitudes, but always be mindful of midday heat and ensure your dog has access to water.

Q: Are there any easy dog-friendly hikes suitable for beginners or shorter walks?
A: While many trails in Cadí-Moixeró are moderate to difficult, you can find shorter, more accessible options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31873002" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellar de n'Hug – Font dels Torrents loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.5 km route that offers a pleasant walk without extreme challenges, making it suitable for a relaxed outing with your dog.

Q: Can I find more challenging dog-friendly routes in Cadí-Moixeró?
A: Yes, Cadí-Moixeró offers several challenging dog-friendly routes for experienced hikers and their canine companions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6957900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellar de n'Hug – Coll de la Creueta loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 17.8 km trail with significant elevation gain, providing a rewarding experience for those seeking a strenuous adventure.

Q: Are there dog-friendly cafes or restaurants near the trails in Cadí-Moixeró?
A: Many villages surrounding Cadí-Moixeró, such as Castellar de n'Hug, Prullans, and La Seu d'Urgell, have establishments that welcome dogs, especially in outdoor seating areas. It's always a good idea to call ahead to confirm their pet policy before visiting.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on dog-friendly trails in Cadí-Moixeró?
A: The terrain in Cadí-Moixeró is diverse, ranging from lush pine forests and meadows to rocky alpine environments. You'll encounter well-maintained paths, forest tracks, and some steeper, more rugged sections, particularly on higher-altitude routes. Ensure your dog's paws are conditioned for varied surfaces.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly trails that feature waterfalls or interesting water features?
A: Absolutely! The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6956087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellar de n'Hug – Source of the Llobregat River loop&lt;/a&gt; is a fantastic option, leading you to the scenic Source of the Llobregat River. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31869780" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llosa Valley – Molí del Salt Waterfall loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you to the beautiful Molí del Salt Waterfall, offering refreshing spots for you and your dog.

Q: What notable landmarks or viewpoints can I visit with my dog in Cadí-Moixeró?
A: Cadí-Moixeró is rich in natural landmarks. You can explore areas near the iconic Pedraforca massif, or visit refuges like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/949301" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lluís Estasen Refuge&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/232972" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Niu de l'Àliga Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, which often serve as starting points for hikes with panoramic views. The geological formations around Coll de Creus also offer unique sights.

Q: Is parking readily available at the trailheads for dog-friendly hikes?
A: Parking is generally available at or near the main trailheads in Cadí-Moixeró, especially in popular starting points like Castellar de n'Hug, Prullans, and La Seu d'Urgell. However, during peak season, it's advisable to arrive early to secure a spot, particularly for well-known routes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Cadí-Moixeró?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Reviewers often praise the stunning mountain scenery, the diverse landscapes from forests to high peaks, and the well-maintained trails that offer a true immersion in nature. The sense of tranquility and the opportunity to explore iconic Catalan Pyrenees features are frequently highlighted.
tips: The El Collell pass marks the intersection of several well-known routes in the area and is located at about 1800 meters high.
Mountain of 2,548 meters located in the Sierra del Cadí
A magical place on the well-known Cavalls del Vent route, rising from the Prat d'aguilo refuge in the Sierra del Casi

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Paso dels Gosolans and Comabona trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features diverse alpine terrain, including sustained ascents, grassy plains, and rocky ledges. Expect a stony chain leading to the summit of Comabona from Prat d'Aguiló. The route also offers dramatic mountain ranges, sheer cliffs, and deep valleys.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or views along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable points, including the Font de la Roca, El Collell Pass, and the historic Gosolans Pass. From the Comabona Summit (2,548 m), you'll get impressive panoramic views of La Cerdanya and the surrounding mountains, including the north face of Pedraforca.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike is typically from late spring to early autumn. In winter, the ascent to Paso dels Gosolans can be complicated by snow and a 35° incline, potentially requiring an ice ax and crampons until late spring due to technical and exposed sections.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The route often begins near the Prat d'Aguiló refuge. You would typically park in Gósol and then access the trailhead, though specific parking details for Prat d'Aguiló should be checked locally.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in Cadí-Moixeró Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for day hiking on established trails within the Cadí-Moixeró Natural Park. However, it's always good practice to check the official park website or local information centers for any updated regulations or specific area restrictions.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in Cadí-Moixeró Natural Park, it's crucial to keep them on a leash to protect wildlife and respect other hikers. Given the trail's difficulty and technical sections, ensure your dog is fit enough for a challenging alpine hike.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other well-known hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other well-known paths. Notably, it is part of the famous Cavalls del Vent route and also crosses sections of the Camí de Picasso, Camí de l'Últim Càtar, and Camí dels bons homes - Catalunya.

faqs.question: Are there any specific sections of the trail that require extra caution?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail includes technical and exposed sections, particularly during the descent via Pas de Tancalaporta and Pas del Bou. These parts demand careful attention and sure-footedness. The stony chain leading to Comabona also requires focus.

faqs.question: What level of fitness and experience is required for this hike?
faqs.answer: This is considered a "difficult" hike, requiring "very good fitness" and "sure-footedness." It involves significant elevation gain and technical sections, so prior alpine hiking experience and sturdy shoes are highly recommended.

faqs.question: What is the historical significance of the Gosolans Pass?
faqs.answer: The Gosolans Pass is a historically significant route in the Sierra del Cadí, connecting Berguedà with Cerdanya. It was traditionally used by reapers from Gósol, musicians, and smugglers. Famously, Pablo Picasso crossed this pass in 1906, a period that influenced his artistic evolution.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: This challenging hike typically takes around 7 hours and 44 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.
